mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.matched_phenotypes: sensorineural hearing loss, retinitis pigmentosa, type 2 diabetes mellitus, obesity, renal cysts, hepatomegaly, pancreatic insufficiency, cardiac defects, respiratory distress, and neuropathies.
proteinuria, angioedema, hypohydrosis, corneal opacities, hyperhidrosis, arrhythmias, myocardial infarction, stroke, and chronic kidney disease.
neurological symptoms (ataxia, dystonia, vertical supranuclear gaze palsy), liver involvement (splenomegaly, cirrhosis), lung disease (interstitial lung disease), and cherry red spot.
epilepsy, intellectual disability, skin abnormalities (facial angiofibromas or fibrous nodules, ash leaf maculae, shagreen patches, periungual fibromas), and ocular findings (retinal astrocytomas).
joint hypermobility, easy bruising, vascular fragility leading to rupture, intestinal complications such as hernias and malabsorption, and scoliosis.
short stature, skeletal deformities including kyphoscoliosis, odontoid hypoplasia, narrow thorax, and mitral valve prolapse.
peripheral polyneuropathy with axonal degeneration, cataracts, anosmia, and acanthocytes in blood smear.
tall stature, long fingers, disproportionately long arms and legs, ectopic lentis, pectus excavatum, and mitral valve prolapse.
coarse facies, hepatosplenomegaly, growth retardation, bone changes, and cognitive decline.
